title: Yahoo OAuth2 keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Yahoo OAuth2 access token detected. This token provides authenticated access to Yahoo APIs including Mail, Calendar, Contacts, and other Yahoo services. Unauthorized access can lead to account takeover, email access, personal data theft, and privacy violations. Immediately revoke this token from your Yahoo developer console and rotate credentials using secure environment variables.

Yahoo OAuth2 client secret detected. This secret is used to authenticate your application with Yahoo OAuth2 services. Exposing client secrets enables unauthorized application impersonation, API abuse, and potential access to user data through your Yahoo application. Revoke immediately from Yahoo developer console and implement secure credential storage.

Yahoo OAuth2 client ID detected. This identifier is used to authenticate your application with Yahoo OAuth2 services. While less sensitive than secrets, exposing client IDs can reveal application configuration details and enable targeted attacks against your Yahoo integration. Store securely using environment variables or configuration management.
